items.title: .@_HannahRitchie, our Deputy Editor and Science Outreach Lead, has won the 2026 Unwin Award! The award recognizes “non-fiction writers in the earlier...
items.description_html:
.@_HannahRitchie, our Deputy Editor and Science Outreach Lead, has won the 2026 Unwin Award!

The award recognizes “non-fiction writers in the earlier stages of their careers as authors, whose work is considered to have made a significant contribution to the world.”

It’s awarded for an author’s overall body of work. Hannah has written two books:

– Clearing the Air: A Hopeful Guide to Solving Climate Change in 50 Questions and Answers
– Not the End of the World: Surprising facts, dangerous myths and hopeful solutions for our future on planet Earth

The award’s judging panel praised "Not the End of the World" as “a well-written and revealing book and for its optimistic and data-grounded approach which gives readers hope for the future of the planet.”

The award comes with a £10,000 prize, which Hannah decided to donate to the Against Malaria Foundation.

Congratulations, Hannah!

Read more in the award announcement: https://www.publishers.org.uk/hannah-ritchie-wins-unwin-award-2026/

items.title: RT Lukasz Olejnik: Research shows that Chinese AI can reliably detect software vulnerabilities - and it is cost efficient. Kimi K2.5, an open-weight m...
items.description_html:
RT Lukasz Olejnik
Research shows that Chinese AI can reliably detect software vulnerabilities - and it is cost efficient. Kimi K2.5, an open-weight model was deployed in an agentic framework against Chrome and produced 10 previously unknown zero-days, including two critical sandbox-escape CVEs. The researchers noted it was cheaper to run than Claude Opus 4.6 at that scale. The most important word in the paper isn't "zero-day" -- it's "cheaper"? https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.20801

items.title: RT Johan Fourie: Will AI replace us or relocate our labour ? Yesterday I gave a talk to twenty-five of Stellenbosch University’s Heads of Department ...
items.description_html:
RT Johan Fourie
Will AI replace us or relocate our labour ?

Yesterday I gave a talk to twenty-five of Stellenbosch University’s Heads of Department about artificial intelligence.

When I started preparing the talk a week ago, two of the tools I ended up using did not yet exist. Opus 4.7, the model I use most, was released only last Thursday. Claude Design, which I used to build the slides, appeared the next day. If I had given the same talk a month ago, several of my examples would not have been available. A week from now, parts of this essay will already read as slightly dated.

General-purpose technologies are an old story: the plough, steam, electricity, the computer. Each reallocated labour rather than eliminating it, but each also rewarded some people, places, and organisations sooner than others. The interesting question has never been ‘will it replace us?’ It has always been ‘where does labour go, and who gets there first?’

items.description_html:
RT Megha Patnaik
Happy to see my work in @thewire_in

GST’s key feature: it’s self-enforcing. Firms need invoices to claim credits → they demand compliance from suppliers → formalisation cascades through supply chains.

https://thewire.in/economy/how-the-gst-created-a-formalisation-cascade

from @Ideas4India

Paper
https://meghapatnaik.github.io/research/gst_prowess.pdf

items.title: RT Megha Patnaik: new blog post at @Ideas4India 45% of India’s manufacturing is informal. My work shows GST is slowly changing this: an input credit ...
items.description_html:
RT Megha Patnaik
new blog post at @Ideas4India 45% of India’s manufacturing is informal. My work shows GST is slowly changing this: an input credit system creates a formalisation cascade where firms demand invoices from suppliers & this pulls the entire supply chain slowly into the formal economy


Ideas for India: Value-added tax has a self-enforcing design that generates formalisation cascades through supply chains. Using @_CMIE Prowess data on 12,024 firms for the period 2013-2022, Megha Patnaik @megonomista examines impact of India’s introduction of GST in 2017
https://www.ideasforindia.in/topics/macroeconomics/how-the-gst-created-a-formalisation-cascade

items.title: RT Megha Patnaik: Global Capability Centres are projected to contribute to 3.5% of India's GDP. My new paper argues that GCCs are firm-level reorganis...
items.description_html:
RT Megha Patnaik
Global Capability Centres are projected to contribute to 3.5% of India's GDP. My new paper argues that GCCs are firm-level reorganisation at the global scale: only the most productive firms set up GCCs, trading off lower wages against higher coordination costs of remote work.


CEPR: New CEPR Discussion Paper - DP21294
Services Across Borders: How Firms Organize Remote Work at Scale
Megha Patnaik @megonomista @UniLUISS
https://ow.ly/2gIf50YuL4m
#CEPR_ITRE #EconTwitter

items.title: RT Megha Patnaik: 45% of India's GDP is informal. My new paper shows GST's credit mechanism is pulling firms into the formal economy, not through enfo...
items.description_html:
RT Megha Patnaik
45% of India's GDP is informal. My new paper shows GST's credit mechanism is pulling firms into the formal economy, not through enforcement but because every buyer demands an invoice from the supplier behind it.
Formalisation cascading begins with large firms and grows upstream


CEPR: New CEPR Discussion Paper - DP21163
Self-Enforcing Tax Design and Supply Chain Formalization: Evidence from India’s GST Reform
Megha Patnaik @megonomista @UniLUISS
https://ow.ly/5QK550YfcL1
#CEPR_PE #EconTwitter

items.title: RT Pramod P: Re Finally, there is some truth to the issue that usage of organized sector as proxy for unorganised is not fully valid. E.g. Agriculture...
items.description_html:
RT Pramod P
Re Finally, there is some truth to the issue that usage of organized sector as proxy for unorganised is not fully valid. E.g. Agriculture is independently measured without reference to corporates. Similarly sectors like Construction and Transport while it uses organized sector indicators (Vehicles, Cement, Steel etc) for measurement, these inputs are mostly produced in the organized sector and would be a valid way of measurement.

items.title: RT Pramod P: Re The author's point that usage of CPI instead of WPI for some of the services sectors would cause around 0.3% average less growth would...
items.description_html:
RT Pramod P
Re The author's point that usage of CPI instead of WPI for some of the services sectors would cause around 0.3% average less growth would be right and there is some compensatory offset effects due to higher deflators for Communications equipment manufacturing (electronics prices reduce usually even with an increase in quality) and communication services may lead to some underestimaton.

items.title: RT Pramod P: Re 2. Sectors like Trade and Other services have also significantly reduced in size in the new series proving the point that the old seri...
items.description_html:
RT Pramod P
Re 2. Sectors like Trade and Other services have also significantly reduced in size in the new series proving the point that the old series overstated growth.

items.title: RT Pramod P: Re 1. There is an increase in size in agriculture and Ownership of dwelling (notional entry mostly). This masks the reduction in size of ...
items.description_html:
RT Pramod P
Re 1. There is an increase in size in agriculture and Ownership of dwelling (notional entry mostly). This masks the reduction in size of other industries in household sector. However, the Private Corporate sector includes something called Quasi corporates which are effectively informal sector as well. Private corporate sector has reduced in size as per the new series.

items.title: RT Pramod P: Prof. Goldars one primary argument that the old methodology of using organized sector growth as proxy for informal sector didn't cause mu...
items.description_html:
RT Pramod P
Prof. Goldars one primary argument that the old methodology of using organized sector growth as proxy for informal sector didn't cause much overestimation rests on the case that Household sector size didn't change much even in the new 2022-23 series. However, few caveats:


Ideas for India: Some scholars have put forth that India’s GDP growth rate was overestimated in the old national accounts series with base year 2011-12, due to methodological issues. Goldar refutes these arguments; concludes there was likely no significant overestimation

https://www.ideasforindia.in/topics/macroeconomics/was-indias-growth-rate-overestimated-in-the-old-national-accounts-series

RT India First
How the GST created a formalisation cascade.


Ideas for India: Value-added tax has a self-enforcing design that generates formalisation cascades through supply chains. Using @_CMIE Prowess data on 12,024 firms for the period 2013-2022, Megha Patnaik @megonomista examines impact of India’s introduction of GST in 2017
https://www.ideasforindia.in/topics/macroeconomics/how-the-gst-created-a-formalisation-cascade

items.title: Jefferies on steel 🔍 1. China’s output cuts (-8% YoY) are shrinking exports (-13% YoY in Mar), tightening supply and lifting Asian spreads 2. Conv...
items.description_html:
Jefferies on steel 🔍

1. China’s output cuts (-8% YoY) are shrinking exports (-13% YoY in Mar), tightening supply and lifting Asian spreads

2. Conversion spreads are rebounding from ~15-year lows, with more upside as China balance improves

3. India steel prices +20% CYTD (helped by 12% safeguard duty) and could rise further to ~₹65.8K vs ₹58K spot if global spreads normalize.

4. Earnings upgrades likely for JSW Steel & Tata Steel with strong EPS sensitivity to price gains.

JSW Steel remains the top Buy!

@CNBCTV18News @CNBCTV18Live

items.title: The correct answer here is to not vote at all. Most people in the world won't vote because this is a silly assumption that they will. Three choices: n...
items.description_html:
The correct answer here is to not vote at all. Most people in the world won't vote because this is a silly assumption that they will.

Three choices:
no vote assumed to be red: most ppl are red, survive.
No vote=blue, survive
No vote= unaffected, so survive


Tim Urban: Everyone in the world has to take a private vote by pressing a red or blue button. If more than 50% of people press the blue button, everyone survives. If less than 50% of people press the blue button, only people who pressed the red button survive. Which button would you press?

items.title: India doesn't need a swap line. It has 600bn$ nearly, to sell, if it wants and sadly it doesn't want to. Cad is 50bn to 75bn a year. There is no exit ...
items.description_html:
India doesn't need a swap line. It has 600bn$ nearly, to sell, if it wants and sadly it doesn't want to. Cad is 50bn to 75bn a year.

There is no exit tax. 20% above 10L a year is only for lrs and is advance tax that can be adjusted even against salary tds.

And no mf inv was limited in 2009, not now. We didn't increase limits

Lrs for investment is tiny tiny tiny
Compared to travel. And even education. People are not lining up to take out money other than a few want to believe on twitter

All figments of imagination. We should sell a lot more dollars. And at least now try to build trade in inr.


Somnath Mukherjee: Idiots + imagined facts = Influencers. Ver 101👇

India has stepped into FY27 amid an extremely challenging global landscape marked by escalating geopolitical hostilities. The ongoing conflict in West Asia has resulted in a drastic jump in international energy prices and triggered supply-side constraints. items.title: RT وزارة الخارجية: حضرة صاحبِ الجلالة السلطان هيثم بن طارق المُعظم - حفظه الله ورعاه...
items.description_html:
RT وزارة الخارجية
حضرة صاحبِ الجلالة السلطان هيثم بن طارق المُعظم - حفظه الله ورعاه - يستقبل بقصر البركة العامر، معالي الدكتور سيد عباس عراقجي وزير خارجية الجمهورية الإسلامية الإيرانية ، جرى خلال المقابلة التشاور حول مستجدات الأوضاع في المنطقة، وجهود الوساطة والمساعي الرامية إلى إنهاء النزاعات، حيث اطّلع جلالةُ السلطان على وجهات نظر الجانب الإيراني حيال تلك التّطورات.

فيما استمع معالي الوزير إلى مرئيات جلالته بشأن سُبل الدّفع بهذه الجهود بما يعزز فرص التوصل إلى حلول سياسيّة مُستدامة ويحدّ من تداعيات الأزمات على شعوب المنطقة، مؤكدًا جلالةُ السلطان على أهمية تغليب لغة الحوار والدّبلوماسية في معالجة القضايا، بما يُسهم في ترسيخ دعائم السّلام.

من جانبه أعرب معالي الدكتور وزير الخارجية الإيراني عن تقدير بلاده لمواقف سلطنة عُمان بقيادة جلالتِه في دعم جهود الحوار وتعزيز مساعي الأمن والاستقرار في المنطقة، خاصة في ظل التحدّيات الإقليميّة الرّاهنة.

items.title: RT Treasury Department: Today, Treasury’s Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctioned Hengli Petrochemical (Dalian) Refinery Co., Ltd., a Chin...
items.description_html:
RT Treasury Department
Today, Treasury’s Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctioned Hengli Petrochemical (Dalian) Refinery Co., Ltd., a China-based independent teapot refinery. China-based teapot refineries continue to play a vital role in sustaining Iran’s oil economy, and Hengli Petrochemical is one of Iran’s largest customers, having purchased billions of dollars’ worth of Iranian petroleum. Additionally today, OFAC is targeting around 40 shipping firms and vessels associated with Iran’s shadow fleet. Economic Fury continues to disrupt Tehran’s ability to generate the revenue that enables the regime’s reckless terrorist activities.

items.title: In November, U.S. exports grew 6.1% YoY, slowing from 15.8% in October, while imports rose 6.5% month-on-month but stayed lower YoY. Exports to China ...
items.description_html:
In November, U.S. exports grew 6.1% YoY, slowing from 15.8% in October, while imports rose 6.5% month-on-month but stayed lower YoY.

Exports to China fell for the 11th consecutive month, with China’s share of U.S. exports dropping to 3.8%. Exports to Taiwan and India rose YoY.

items.title: BREAKING: Iran through Pakistani mediators has given the US a new proposal for reaching a deal on the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and ending the...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: Iran through Pakistani mediators has given the US a new proposal for reaching a deal on the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and ending the war, per Axios.

Details include:

1. Nuclear negotiations are postponed for a later stage under the deal

2. President Trump is set to hold a meeting with his top national security and foreign policy team on Monday to discuss negotiations

3. President Trump has signaled he wants to continue the US “blockade” of the Strait of Hormuz

4. President Trump said the Iranian position led him to cancel a trip by his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan

Indirect US-Iran talks appear to still be ongoing.

items.title: Tech dominance is now a global trend: The information technology sector now reflects 85% of Taiwan's stock market cap, the most among major world mark...
items.description_html:
Tech dominance is now a global trend:

The information technology sector now reflects 85% of Taiwan's stock market cap, the most among major world markets.

This is followed by South Korea and the Netherlands, at 57.2% and 54.6%, respectively.

The US is ranked 4th, with IT making up 32.7% of the S&P 500's total market value.

Including communication services, technology stocks reflect a near-record 45% of the US stock market, still below the top 3 countries.

Meanwhile, the weight of global tech stocks as a % of the total global market ex-US stands at ~11.0%, the highest since 2021 but below the 14% peak during the 2000 Dot-Com Bubble.

Global equity markets have rarely been this concentrated in a single sector.

items.title: White collar employment is sharply declining: The number of the S&P 500 employees fell -400,000 in 2025, to 28.1 million, posting its first annual dec...
items.description_html:
White collar employment is sharply declining:

The number of the S&P 500 employees fell -400,000 in 2025, to 28.1 million, posting its first annual decline since 2016.

This follows 8 consecutive years of uninterrupted employment growth, adding over +3.0 million jobs in total.

The decline was driven by UPS, $UPS, Oracle, $ORCL, Amazon, $AMZN, Meta, $META, Intel, $INTC, and Microsoft, $MSFT, as corporations raced to cut costs and redirect spending toward AI.

In 2026, layoffs are set to continue with Amazon cutting ~16,000 corporate jobs, Meta slashing ~8,000 positions, and Microsoft offering voluntary buyouts to ~8,750 employees.

Corporate America is cutting jobs at an accelerating pace.

items.title: Food inflation is set to accelerate in the US: Average inflation for food and beverage companies surged +7.9% YoY in March, the biggest jump in at lea...
items.description_html:
Food inflation is set to accelerate in the US:

Average inflation for food and beverage companies surged +7.9% YoY in March, the biggest jump in at least 12 months.

This is up +373 basis points from +4.2% in February.

The largest jump was recorded in tomatoes at +102% YoY, vegetables at +90%, and diesel at +88%.

This was largely driven by higher fuel costs, meaning the full impact of rising fertilizer and plastics prices has not yet been reflected.

Meanwhile, urea prices, the world's most widely used nitrogen fertilizer, have doubled since February, to ~$900 per metric ton, the highest since the 2022.

Fertilizer costs are rapidly rising for farmers and will eventually translate into higher wholesale food prices before landing on grocery store shelves.

Food inflation is set to accelerate.

items.title: Investors are flooding into equity ETFs at a record pace: Average daily US equity ETF inflows surged to a record +$7.5 billion in the first 3 weeks of...
items.description_html:
Investors are flooding into equity ETFs at a record pace:

Average daily US equity ETF inflows surged to a record +$7.5 billion in the first 3 weeks of April.

This is up +153% compared to the March average of +$2.9 billion.

By comparison, the full year 2025 average was +$3.7 billion, meaning April is running at more than DOUBLE last year's pace.

As a result, total US equity ETF inflows have exceeded +$100 billion since the March 30th bottom.

Investors are pouring more capital into equity funds than ever.

items.title: This is unprecedented: The semiconductor sector, $SOX, has rallied +150% YoY, the strongest 1-year increase since the 2000 Dot-Com Bubble. This even s...
items.description_html:
This is unprecedented:

The semiconductor sector, $SOX, has rallied +150% YoY, the strongest 1-year increase since the 2000 Dot-Com Bubble.

This even surpasses the +100% surge during the recovery from the 2020 pandemic.

$SOX has now closed higher for 18 consecutive trading sessions, the longest streak in history, overtaking the previous record of 15 days set in 2014.

Historically, the performance of semiconductor stocks has been a leading indicator for the ISM Manufacturing PMI Index.

If the relationship still holds, the Manufacturing PMI could surge to as high as 60 points, the highest level since 2021.

Excluding 2021, that would be the highest reading in 22 years.

US manufacturing may be on the verge of historic expansion.

items.title: Hedge funds are taking profits on tech stocks: Hedge funds posted their largest reduction in US information technology exposure since July 2024 last w...
items.description_html:
Hedge funds are taking profits on tech stocks:

Hedge funds posted their largest reduction in US information technology exposure since July 2024 last week.

This also marks the 3rd largest weekly exit from the sector in at least 5 years.

The move was driven by long sales outpacing short covers at a ratio of 1.9 to 1.0.

Exposure was cut in nearly every subsector, led by Software, Semiconductors, Tech Hardware, and Communications Equipment.

Despite the reduction, hedge fund allocation in tech remains elevated, at 20.6% of total US gross market value.

This is higher than 92% of the time over the last year and higher than 98% of the time over the last 5 years.

Hedge funds are realizing gains after a historic run.

items.title: 2026 has been historic: The Permanent Portfolio is on track to gain +26% in 2026, its best annual return since 1933. It consists of equal portions of ...
items.description_html:
2026 has been historic:

The Permanent Portfolio is on track to gain +26% in 2026, its best annual return since 1933.

It consists of equal portions of US stocks, 10-year Treasury bonds, commodities, and cash.

The portfolio is also on track for its 3rd higest annual outperformance versus the traditional 60/40 portfolio in over a century, with only 1946 and 1973 producing stronger relative returns.

This comes as the portfolio is up +8% year-to-date, with commodities leading at +28%.

A bull market in commodities is fueling alternative portfolios.

items.title: Americans are concerned about their jobs: In April, 64% of US consumers expected unemployment to be higher over the next 12 months, near the highest r...
items.description_html:
Americans are concerned about their jobs:

In April, 64% of US consumers expected unemployment to be higher over the next 12 months, near the highest reading on record.

This percentage has DOUBLED since June 2022, when 32% of Americans expected unemployment to rise.

By comparison, the 2008 Financial Crisis peak and the November 2025 high were both 69%.

Historically, such high pessimism has never happened outside of recessions.

Meanwhile, the unemployment rate stands at 4.3%, well below the 2010 peak of 10.6% and the 1983 high of 11.4%.

Job market expectations are at recession levels.

items.title: BREAKING: Foreign holdings of US Treasuries surged +$200 billion in February, to a record $9.49 trillion. Of that total, $7.76 trillion, or 84%, consi...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: Foreign holdings of US Treasuries surged +$200 billion in February, to a record $9.49 trillion.

Of that total, $7.76 trillion, or 84%, consists of long-term Treasury securities, with the remainder held in short-term Treasury bills.

Over the last 12 months, holdings have risen +$587 billion.

Japan, the largest foreign holder, raised its stockpile by +$14 billion, to $1.24 trillion, the highest since February 2022.

This marks Japan's 13th monthly purchase of the last 14 months, as Japanese institutions continue chasing higher yields overseas.

The UK, the 2nd-largest holder, posted a +$17 billion increase, to $897 billion, the 2nd-highest on record.

China, the 3rd-largest owner, trimmed its stockpile by -$1 billion, to $693 billion.

Foreign demand for US debt remains strong.

items.title: RT Jim Bianco: This story produced a red screaming headline on Bloomberg. *IRAN OFFERS US NEW PROPOSAL TO REOPEN STRAIT OF HORMUZ: AXIOS The markets e...
items.description_html:
RT Jim Bianco
This story produced a red screaming headline on Bloomberg.

*IRAN OFFERS US NEW PROPOSAL TO REOPEN STRAIT OF HORMUZ: AXIOS

The markets excitedly pumped on the headline.

The problem is that this story was public 11 hours earlier.
https://x.com/BabakVahdad/status/2048411197930709497?s=20

Effectively, this is a totally unrealistic proposal. Still, the administration waited 11 hours until markets were open to hype so-called "good news" by calling an American (or Israeli) reporter to say "A DEAL IS COMING!" and breathlessly include "We'll meet in the situation room to discuss."

What is amazing is how desperate the market wants this to be the case, and how it bounces every time it happens.


Barak Ravid: NEW: Iran gave the U.S. a new proposal for reaching a deal on the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and lifting the naval blockade first, and postponing nuclear negotiations for a later stage. My story on @axios

items.title: IEX Concall Updates Coal Exchange opportunities: Large demand on buy side and sell side with multiple sellers and merchant mines. Govt pushing for E-a...
items.description_html:
IEX Concall Updates
Coal Exchange opportunities:
Large demand on buy side and sell side with multiple sellers and merchant mines.
Govt pushing for E-auction coal to trade through exchanges.
In the Beginning stages, buyers will have to make arrangements to lift the coal themself. Price discovery, coal quality to be ensured.
MCX also filed for Coal exchange but only derivatives, IEX to be spot market.
TAM can be told only after final regulations.

Operations side:
More than 70% trading is through API. Price discovery is through algorithm and using AI for only creating new products and operational improvement.

Gas exchange:
Volumes not as good as expected, due to middle east crisis. Q1 we may not grow, but definitely in Q2 we will see growth. Domestic + other suppliers too trading here.
Deadline to reduce stake in IGX extended to Dec 31st 2026.

Market Coupling:

When asked for a timeline, management says “can't say when final notification will be issued”.
On the new draft about Grid India deciding price, management says “situation is fluid”.
“They might review this decision or may not go ahead at all.”
“All exchanges have their own software. Grid India has to create infrastructure, software and connect all these exchanges. Bids will be collected and submitted to Grid India. I don't think so any value creation is happening.
No benefit of coupling”
As for trading participant’s response to coupling, no change in their working. Customer loyalty is very important. We interact with them, teach them how to use software, data analytics, faster bidding, settlement support, so a lot of value addition from IEX. I am sure we will retain our customers. Even if coupling happens, we will retain our market share. Liquidity will continue. We don't expect any significant margin drop.
RTM no case of market coupling anywhere. Tight timeline 15 minute and 5 minutes time block. Not possible to implement.
Renewable suppliers and battery suppliers number increasing everyday.

FY27 volume growth will be maintained at 15-20% growth. This year will have high demand.

CFD contracts pilot launch for 500MW fixed price battery capacity.

items.title: Finally some good progress on Market Coupling - Round Robin system is no more - Grid India takes Operator status to match bids as a neutral party - Gr...
items.description_html:
Finally some good progress on Market Coupling

- Round Robin system is no more
- Grid India takes Operator status to match bids as a neutral party
- Grid India has 6 months to come up with the Market coupling algorithm similar to Europe's Day Ahead Market
- Post 6 months, then after everyone's approval they have to build the systems, the infrastructure, cybersecurity protection etc etc

Looks like this should take 12 to 18months to see some value

This is assuming IEX does not put new cases on the order to stall

Now contrary to popular belief - Market Coupling like the EU is actually good

- EU trading Market boomed post market Coupling
- The industry leader EPEX Spot who was a market leader before coupling still has most of the volume
- EU has only 2 players taking all the market share with Nord Pool being 2nd

IEX
- Yes market coupling without round Robin is good, but market coupling itself will take some market share
- At best market share should dip from 99% to 75%.
- At best post market coupling they would stall revenues for 1 year
- Post market coupling the regulator will boost Power trading a lot
- Volume will rise
- News instruments like CfD coming to power exchanges

Verdict
Short term IEX will be volatile
For long term investors the game will start post Market Coupling

Today Indias largest Power exchange is at close to a billion dollars.

Disc - Invested in IEX at Lower Levels. Not a buy or sell reco

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: HSBC on Shriram Fin Buy, TP Rs 1200 4QFY26 earnings beat was driven by strong operating cost control, resulting in sharp expansion in ...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
HSBC on Shriram Fin
Buy, TP Rs 1200
4QFY26 earnings beat was driven by strong operating cost control, resulting in sharp expansion in RoAs
Uncertain macro environment and weaker monsoon would be key monitorables for growth and asset quality outlook
Lower AUM growth CAGR estimates to 16% over FY2026-28 (from 18%) amidst expectations of weaker monsoon and slowdown in vehicle sales.
However, this impact is more than offset by lower operating cost assumptions over FY2027-28

Nomura on Shriram Fin
Buy, TP Rs 1200
Raising caution
Management aims for 18% loan growth over medium term but has suggested a 15-18% range for FY27F
8% beat on 4Q26 net profit supported by lower-than-expected opex & credit costs
Risks in FY27F: possible fuel price hike, tepid monsoon predication, MSME disruptions
Focus on new vehicle segment given expected 100bp reduction in cost of funds

CLSA on Shriram Fin
O-P, TP Rs 1150
Reported a 3% PBT beat, driven by lower-than-expected opex, while NII was a marginal miss.
AUM growth of 15% was in line with trend in prior quarters
With MUFG capital coming in, AUM growth should accelerate in FY27 to high teens.
NIM was largely steady QoQ, after two quarters of meaningful NIM improvement
One key driver of NIM improvement – reduction of excess balance sheet liquidity – is not behind us.
Liquidity on balance sheet is 7% of assets vs. 10% in prior quarter, as do not expect it to decline further.
Asset quality was largely in line with credit costs of 1.9%
Net slippage ratio of 1.8% was higher sequentially but lower than that in 4QFY25.

MS on Shriram Fin
OW, TP Rs 1250
Beat vs. consensus was led by loperating costs (even after adjusting for one-offs).
9M credit cost was significantly below historical average.
Hence, had anticipated potential management overlays and built in higher credit costs in 4Q.
Stressed asset formation was higher QoQ but lower YoY and within estimate; this was higher in 2HF25 as well – best to look on a four-quarter rolling basis.
NII beat of 1%; reported NIM expanded 3bp QoQ, to 8.61%.
Adjusted PPOP (ex change in commission expense accounting) beat by 5%.
Credit cost of 1.9% (+7bp QoQ, -52bp YoY) beat 2.30% estimate, driving the 20% adjusted PAT beat vs. estimate (beat VA consensus by 4%).

Macquarie on Shriram Fin
O-P, TP Rs 1220
4Q FY26: Opex control drives beat on PAT estimates
Conservative margin outlook as focus shifts toward growth
Credit cost under wraps, but remains a key monitorable
Management struck a cautious tone on FY27 auto industry growth but targets 18% AUM growth, led by PVs & CVs, while MSME segment might see some moderation

Kotak Inst Eqt on Shriram Fin
ADD, TP Rs 1100
Reported a stable performance in 4QFY26.
Mindful of macro challenges to small businesses, CV operators and its borrowers at large, company will likely tread carefully in the next few months
Completion of the MUFG deal and credit rating upgrade set the stage for harnessing liability-side advantages
@CNBCTV18News @_RituSingh

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: HSBC on IndusInd BK Buy, TP Raised to Rs 1100 4QFY26 earnings performance underscores gradual course correction being undertaken by ma...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
HSBC on IndusInd BK
Buy, TP Raised to Rs 1100
4QFY26 earnings performance underscores gradual course correction being undertaken by management
Increase FY27/28e EPS estimates by 20/10%, respectively, to build in stronger loan growth, margins, and lower credit costs
Key positives
(i) A significant reduction in gross and net slippages as well as write-offs, leading to a significant reduction in credit costs. We believe slippages in MFI are still a tad
high and should decline further.
(ii) pick-up in disbursements in vehicle, microfinance, affordable housing, SME and the rationalisation of low yielding corporate loans indicates
that IIB is gearing up to improve loan growth
(iii) Most of the top management hiring has been completed.
(iv) Appointment of two Executive Directors and two Independent Directors to strengthen Board of Directors, subject to further approvals.

Jefferies on IndusInd BK
Buy, TP Rs 1100
IIB's performance in Mar-Qtr is encouraging with earnings ahead of estimates aided by lower credit cost and higher treasury gains.
Profit of Rs5bn was ahead of est.
Leadership team & Board resets are largely done, quality of collaboration will be key.
Hence, expect uptick in growth & improvement in profitability
Lift est. & upsides can come from better treasury & lower opex
Val at 1x FY27 adj PB is attractive

UBS on IndusInd BK
Sell. TP Rs 730
PAT beat due to lower credit costs and Opex
Loan book down 0.5% QoQ (vs -2.6% in Q3); LDR drops by 160bp
Bank aims to grow at system levels in FY27; exit FY27 ROA target of ~1%
BK trades at 1x FY27E PBV & believe stock is fully valued given weak growth.

Bernstein on IndusInd BK
O-P, TP Rs 1000
4Q performance reflected early but visible signs of stabilization, with improvement across asset quality, balance sheet trends, and profitability
Sharp decline in slippages and credit costs provided meaningful support to earnings, while deposit growth turned positive and the contraction in advances remained contained, indicating a gradual normalization in business momentum.
Although core operating performance remains relatively subdued, management retained its medium-term guidance on loan growth, asset quality, and profitability, suggesting confidence in a steady recovery path rather than a sharp turnaround.

Kotak Inst Eqt on IndusInd Bk
Reduce, TP Rs 800
Reported a mixed quarter, with lower slippages (2.3%) translating into lower credit costs, albeit still elevated at about 2%, while RoE remained weak at 3% due to soft operating profitability. With balance-sheet surprises largely behind us, attention shifts to RoE recovery, requiring a careful balance between revenue traction, gradual credit cost moderation and tight cost control. Maintain REDUCE as RoE recovery path underpinning any re-rating appears slower than anticipated from here.
@CNBCTV18News @_RituSingh @latha_venkatesh

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: Nomura on Axis Bk Buy, TP Rs 1560 4QFY26: Credit cost beat salvages a soft core quarter Below estimates on PPOP, in line on PAT Strong...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
Nomura on Axis Bk
Buy, TP Rs 1560
4QFY26: Credit cost beat salvages a soft core quarter
Below estimates on PPOP, in line on PAT
Strong improvement in asset quality; robust loan growth driven by corporate segment
Forecast RoA/RoE of 1.6-1.7%/14-15% over FY27-28F, & a strong earnings CAGR of 23% over FY26-28F
In this context, valuations look attractive at 1.4x FY28F P/B

UBS on Axis Bk
Buy, TP Raised to Rs 1620
Inline Q4 PAT: Mixed qtr with one-offs, lower PPOP & lower core credit cost
Loan growth accelerates to 18.5% YoY; LDR up c40bp QoQ
Management reiterate through cycle NIM guidance of c3.8%
Stock trades at 1.7x FY27E P/BV (vs 1.65x/2.x for HDFCB/ICICI) & risk-reward appears favourable

Kotak Inst Eqt on Axis BK
Buy, TP Rs 1600
Axis Bank delivered flat earnings, with operating profit down 7% yoy, reflecting higher one-off provisions linked to global tensions, partly offset by a lower tax rate.
Revenue growth remained muted, despite strong loan growth due to weak non-interest income, while NIM was unchanged qoq.
A significant relative re-rating versus peers has already occurred, making further outperformance materially harder to sustain.

Bernstein on Axis Bank
O-P, TP Rs 1600
Healthy 4Q, with strong loan growth led by wholesale segments & visible improvement in asset quality supporting overall profitability
While margin pressure persisted & weaker fee income along with trading losses weighed on operating income, pace of NIM decline moderated, suggesting that sharpest phase of margin normalization may be behind. Lower slippages and credit costs, alongside prudent contingent provisioning funded through one-off tax gains, helped drive RoA improvement to 1.58%, reflecting a healthy quarter.
Axis Bank’s relatively optimal capital positioning—compared with more overcapitalized balance sheets of ICICI Bank, HDFC Bank, and KMB—continues to provide a tactical advantage, enabling it to deliver a 15% RoE (higher than HDFC Bank) despite operating with a lower RoA than peers.
@CNBCTV18News @_RituSingh @latha_venkatesh

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: UBS on IDFC First Bk Sell TP Rs 70 PAT impacted by multiple one-offs; Asset quality improves sequentially Loan growth remains steady, ...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
UBS on IDFC First Bk
Sell TP Rs 70
PAT impacted by multiple one-offs; Asset quality improves sequentially
Loan growth remains steady, margins expand 17bp QoQ
Management guided for improvement in deposits; Margins to remain stable
Cut margins assumption & fee income but also lower opex
Further lower credit cost by 5-10bp for FY27/28E & cut EPS estimate by c9% for FY27 while it is largely unchanged for FY28E
STK trades at 1.1x FY27E P/BV.

CLSA on IDFC First BK
Hold, TP cut to Rs 73 from Rs 82
4Q PBT of Rs1.9bn, a beat compared to estimate
While core operating profit (adj. for treasury and one-offs) was largely in line, credit costs came in better than expectations
This was driven by a 70bp sequential reduction in gross slippage ratio to 2.6% (best in several quarters).
Deposit growth was muted (1% QoQ) driven by savings account (SA) rate cuts & Haryana fraud incident
Bank utilised some excess liquidity and was thus able to grow loans 4% QoQ
NIM improved 17bps QoQ to 5.9% driven by lower interest reversals, a lower cost of deposits and some accounting intricacies.
Nevertheless, management is guiding NIM of only 5.75% in FY27
Cut FY27/28CL estimates driven by a c.10% cut to loan growth & NIM cuts as guided by management.

Nomura on IDFC First BK
Buy, TP Rs 85
4Q26 – Resilient quarter amid fraud-related overhang
Margin, asset quality surprise positively
Deposit growth to pick up pace; asset quality improves
Strong delivery on margins and controlled opex led to core-PPOP beat

Jefferies on IDFC First BK
Buy, TP Rs 82
For Mar-Qtr, IDFC Bank's profit of Rs3bn was ahead of estimates due to lower credit costs and higher fees.
If not for write-off for deposit fraud case, profit would have been much higher.
Loan growth could remain at c.20%, but derisking of mix and rise in funding costs may limit margins
Trim estimates by 3% to factor in results, but profitability should see marked improvement with lower credit costs; ROA to reach 1% by FY28E.
@CNBCTV18News @_RituSingh @latha_venkatesh

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: CLSA on RBL BL Hold, TP Rs 320 A tough quarter with 4QFY26 PBT c.20% below estimate Miss was driven by both PPOP and credit costs. On ...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
CLSA on RBL BL
Hold, TP Rs 320
A tough quarter with 4QFY26 PBT c.20% below estimate
Miss was driven by both PPOP and credit costs.
On PPOP front, while loan growth was good, NII growth was tepid.
NII grew just 1% QoQ, despite double-digit loan growth, driven by a 50bp decline in loan yields.
Credit costs were sequentially unchanged at 2.5%, driven by continued pressure on asset quality in credit cards
Only good thing in quarter was growth – both on loans and deposits.
Deposit growth of 16% QoQ had some transient, quarter-end deposit flows – adjusted for that, it would have been 12% QoQ
Loan book also grew 11% QoQ.

CITI on RBL BK
Buy TP Rs 390
RoA held flat QoQ at 0.55%, missing estimates due to a 22bps NIM contraction to 4.41% (vs CitiE of 4.64%) & elevated credit costs of 2.5%.
Yield compression of 49bps was driven by repo repricing, liquidity drag, asset mix shifts, and interest reversals.
Mgmt. guides NIMs to remain flattish in 1QFY27 before gradually recovering, supported by capital infusion.
Credit card stress persists — elevated slippages/write-offs are expected for two more quarters before normalizing in 2H — though JLG stress has meaningfully eased.
Advances grew a strong 11% QoQ, led by wholesale and secured retail, while unsecured retail remained subdued.
While growth trajectory was encouraging with contained cost, NIM and credit cost pressures weighed on RoA.
FY27E earnings revised down 3% on lower NIM and fee assumptions.
Key catalysts: ENBD equity infusion and credit cost normalization in 2H.

@CNBCTV18News @_RituSingh @latha_venkatesh

items.title: Just because you have done in depth analysis of a stock and industry , doesn't mean that it is a good company from a return profile point of view and ...
items.description_html:
Just because you have done in depth analysis of a stock and industry , doesn't mean that it is a good company from a return profile point of view and you get attached to

I used to make this mistake too few years back , do not get attached to the thesis or think that the effort you put into a company has any correlation with the return profile of the stock , keep the process and expectations basket separate, build a process that cuts the ideas when need to be and brings them back when cycle turns , you are not owed any returns because you read a company well !

items.title: Overall Construction cost increase between FY23-26 is ~11%, implying <3% annualized inflation - very manageable for developers Labour is the largest c...
items.description_html:
Overall Construction cost increase between FY23-26 is ~11%, implying <3% annualized inflation - very manageable for developers

Labour is the largest cost component (~37% of total cost) & remains structurally stable due to strong migrant supply availability

Key commodities like steel, electricals have seen some increase, but several components like flooring, plumbing, RMC & lifts have declined

Construction cost typically forms ~25-45% of sales price, so impact on project profitability remains limited

Longer project execution cycle (3-4 years) allows developers to absorb volatility across phases & manage procurement timing efficiently

Existing ready & under construction inventory acts as a natural hedge against commodity price volatility

Construction cost inflation currently poses low risk to margins for Organized developers

items.title: RT Parimal: He worked in a lab so small & hot that his sweat would often ruin his notes. He did not build a bomb/a satellite, but he solved a puzzle t...
items.description_html:
RT Parimal
He worked in a lab so small & hot that his sweat would often ruin his notes. He did not build a bomb/a satellite, but he solved a puzzle that was killing millions. In 1959, from a Silo in Calcutta, Sambhunath De discovered the secret pump that drains human life. He is the father of ORS: the ghost behind the most successful medical intervention in history. He was nominated for the Nobel by the world’s greatest giants, yet he died in 1985 traveling by local bus, unrecognized by the very people whose children he had saved.

Born in 1915 in a small village in West Bengal, S.N. De did not come from a family of elite scientists. He worked at the Nil Ratan Sircar (NRS) Medical College, Calcutta. While elite scientists were building rockets, De was working in a tiny, cramped lab with barely any ventilation. He did not have high-end sensors. He used his own intuition & rudimentary tools to study how certain invisible forces acted on human cells.

S.N. De solved the mystery of Cholera, but in a way that was pure Fluid Physics & Biophysics. For a century, the world thought Cholera was a blood infection. In 1959, in his tiny lab, De proved it was a toxin that attacked the fluid-transport mechanisms of the gut. He discovered the Cholera Toxin (CT). He demonstrated how the toxin created a pump that sucked water out of cells, a masterclass in osmotic pressure & molecular transport.

This discovery is the direct reason why ORS (Oral Rehydration Salt) exists. If we/anyone we know has ever been saved by a packet of ORS, we owe our life to S.N. De.

In 1954, Nobel Laureate Joshua Lederberg wrote to the Nobel Committee saying that S.N. De’s work was worthy of the prize. He was nominated multiple times, but like many Indian scientists, he was a Ghost in a colony. The prize never came.

In 1978, the Nobel Foundation organized a symposium on Cholera. They realized the man who started it all, S.N. De, was still alive in Calcutta. They invited him, & he arrived at the high-end gala in a simple suit, looking like a retired clerk.

He was a man of aggressive humility. He lived in a small house, traveled by local buses, & never sought patents for his discovery. He wanted the Signal (the cure) to be free for the world. His own family knew him as a dedicated doc who went to the lab every day. They had no idea that Nobel Prize winners in Europe & America were referencing his 1959 paper as 1 of the most important scientific documents of the 20th century.

items.title: RT Nithin Kamath: I recently had dinner with Dr Devi Shetty, the founder of Narayana Hospitals. For those who don't know him, he's the guy who figured...
items.description_html:
RT Nithin Kamath
I recently had dinner with Dr Devi Shetty, the founder of Narayana Hospitals. For those who don't know him, he's the guy who figured out how to do open heart surgery for a few hundred dollars when the same procedure costs a bomb in the US. Narayana has 18,000 beds across India, and if you ask most middle-class people in Bangalore about it, they'll speak highly of it.

There was one thing I kept thinking about over and over again after meeting him.

Narayana's market cap is around ₹38,000 crore. Now compare that to pretty much any half-decent financial services business in India, and it'll be valued more than that, including Zerodha. A brokerage, worth more than a hospital chain, that has probably saved hundreds of thousands of lives.

I get the arguments. If you're a fund manager/analyst, you can immediately explain it away using margins, capex, asset-light vs asset-heavy, and all that, and I'm not saying the market is wrong.

But it's still a strange world we've built, where the businesses closest to money get valued the highest, and the ones doing the hard and essential things get priced like boring utilities. A hospital carries physical infrastructure, enormous liability, thin margins and the actual weight of keeping people alive. And somehow that's worth less than a platform for buying and selling stocks.

I don't have a clean take on this. All of this just felt odd.

Ps: Nothing here is investment advice. For that, go to @zerodhavarsity

items.title: RT Manan S. Bhatt: "Ambushed" #UntoldStoryArmy During a military training course in Mhow, India, the 1962 war with China broke out. My course was canc...
items.description_html:
RT Manan S. Bhatt
"Ambushed"
#UntoldStoryArmy

During a military training course in Mhow, India, the 1962 war with China broke out. My course was cancelled, and everyone returned to their units. However, I received special orders to stay put and await further instructions. Two days later, my posting to Jammu and Kashmir arrived – with immediate reporting required.

Having driven my car to Mhow, I had no choice but to take it back to Punjab, leave it there, and then travel on to J&K. Pressed for time, I decided to drive straight from Mhow to Delhi, a risky decision. One particularly dangerous stretch was between Gwalior and Agra, notorious for bandits and lawlessness.

Traveling alone at night through this area was foolish, but I pressed on in my trusty Hindustan-14. Around midnight, approaching a narrow pass through low hills, I found the road blocked by a fallen tree. It was an ambush. Turning back wasn't an option.

As I stopped, armed men materialized, their faces hidden. They searched me for valuables, taking my watch and wallet before moving on to my car and luggage. However, things took an unexpected turn when someone announced a "Sardar" had arrived.

Every one pulled back a little and there appeared a sturdy young man with a large mustache. As he drew near, he suddenly sprung to attention and gave me a smart salute and said ‘Sahib Ji, tussee ethay ki kar rahey ho.’ (Sir what are you doing here!)

From his voice and his face, latter day mustache notwithstanding, I recognized him. He was my tank gunner and had been discharged after his seven-year tenure of engagement. Those days soldiers were discharged after seven years and given no pension.

I responded to his query and asked him,’ Nahar Singh toon aye ki kam pharya hai (Nahar Singh what is this work you have taken on.) He said, ‘Sahib Ji admi nu kuch na kuch taan karna chaheda hai, welay beth kaay vi ki karna hai.’ (Sir one must do, some or the other work and there is no point in sitting idle!)

I told him, ‘tenu aasi eh taan nahi sakhlaya’ (we did not train you for such activity.) “Sahib ji tohati ambush laney di sikhlai kam ahh rahi hai. Sadi ambush wich koe nuks hai!.” (Sir your training in laying an ambush is proving useful. Do you find any fault with our ambush.)

He responded with a loud chuckle. He addressed his gang and said. “Dekheya sadhi regiment de officer kiney deelair hun, ekelay he rat nu is sarak tay chal rahey hun.” (do you see how brave are the officers of our regiment. They travel alone on this road at night.)

I asked him if his wife knew what work he was doing. To this he answered “Sarian gallaan wife nu thorian dassidian hun.

Oh soch di hai kay main police da kam kar rahaan hain.” (You don’t disclose everything to your wife. She thinks I am working for the police). At this one of them interjected that, “Police whi ehhoo kam kardi hai.” (Police too does this sort of work).

This raised much laughter amongst them. Then he told his men not to just stand watching but to quickly make tea for the sahib. All my stuff was put back in the car, and my watch and wallet returned. After a cup of tea and much bonhomie and handshaking I took leave of Nahar Singh and his gang.

A story by Lt Gen Harwant Singh

items.title: RT Riaz Hamidullah: My dear RAGHU bhai (@RaghuRai1), I won't ever hear your heartfelt voice again over phone, greeting me in immaculate Urdu and filli...
items.description_html:
RT Riaz Hamidullah
My dear RAGHU bhai (@RaghuRai1), I won't ever hear your heartfelt voice again over phone, greeting me in immaculate Urdu and filling void with your signature warmth!

Last (2025) April, within two weeks of joining in Delhi, a call comes to me from him, "Reyaz bhai, I dare address you as 'bhai', hope you would grant me that allowance...". He sends his book, BIRTH OF A NATION, thru his daughter (based on all rare negatives of 1971 War that he misplaced in the pile of his archive).

He was one of my childhood heroes. Many #youth who may not know: in August 1971, a 29-year-old photo-journalist in @TheStatesmanLtd, Raghu Rai, entered #Bangladesh to cover 🇧🇩Liberation War. He shared with me accounts of intense suffering, agony and humiliation of #people that he witnessed in battlefield - villages - Refugee Camps. Raghu bhai narrated to me a chilling account of an ambush when he kept clicking even braving splinters!  

What he saw on the ground reminded him of his childhood when his family had to cross over to India (1947) braving so much of uncertainties. He was barely 5, then.
In 1972, 🇮🇳 Govt. awarded a young Raghu Rai #PadmaShri for his epochal photography on 🇧🇩War.

Since April '25, Raghu bhai embraced me just as a younger brother. A giant of man in the world of #photography #art #aesthetics, each time I would call him, he would greet me on phone almost like hugging me. Words just won't suffice.

Raghu bhai, in eight decades, you touched so many souls, across lands. You didn't belong just to #India but to the world.
Adieu, Raghu bhai. RIP.

@IndiaToday

items.title: RT Jyoti Yadav: On the morning after her wedding in 2008, Asha Devi was told she had to leave Gowardaha village for Mainpuri district in Uttar Pradesh...
items.description_html:
RT Jyoti Yadav
On the morning after her wedding in 2008, Asha Devi was told she had to leave Gowardaha village for Mainpuri district in Uttar Pradesh, nearly 750 kilometres away. She discovered then that her husband Lita Manjhi and father-in-law, Lalo, were bonded labourers — and that she too had been “supplied” as one.     

“Pata chala ki sasur aur pati karja mein khat raha hai (I learnt that they were trapped in debt),” recalled Asha, now 40. “Aise karke humko bhi pakad liya(That’s how I got dragged into it),” she added, sitting on a cot outside her thatched mud hut. 

Forty years ago, her father-in-law had taken a 5,000-rupee loan from Ramashish Prasad Yadav, which he was still paying back in 2008.

My ground report for @TMigrationStory

https://themigrationstory.com/post/the-inheritance-of-debt-bihars-musahars-still-marry-give-birth-in-bondage/

items.title: RT Gaurab Chakrabarti: Stella Chemifa started making fluoride compounds for toothpaste. Today they produce hydrofluoric acid at 12N purity: 99.9999999...
items.description_html:
RT Gaurab Chakrabarti
Stella Chemifa started making fluoride compounds for toothpaste. Today they produce hydrofluoric acid at 12N purity: 99.9999999999%. TSMC, Samsung, Intel, and SK Hynix depend on it to etch every advanced chip on earth. In July 2019, Japan restricted hydrogen fluoride exports to South Korea. Shipments dropped 87.9% in a month. Korea produces over half the world's memory chips, and had no alternative supplier at that purity. South Korea poured billions into domestic production. For the most advanced nodes, they still import from Japan. Getting from five nines to twelve nines took Stella Chemifa decades of process refinement. Every extra nine means ten times fewer contaminants. The global chip supply chain runs through 723 people in Osaka operating on toothpaste chemistry.

items.title: RT Aditya Kalra: ⚖️ 🍔 India's Zomato drops a price parity clause from its contracts after a revolt by restaurants who said it undermined their of...
items.description_html:
RT Aditya Kalra
⚖️ 🍔 India's Zomato drops a price parity clause from its contracts after a revolt by restaurants who said it undermined their offline pricing decisions.

⚖️Zomato was empowered earlier to use mystery shopping to detect any lower-than-app prices, according to Reuters review of these contracts

⚖️Five Lawyers and a former Indian antitrust official who reviewed the clause for @Reuters flagged it as anti-competitive.

⚖️What did the clause say? "Zomato may use various means including but not limited to customer complaints and mystery shopping to identify price disparity between a Restaurant's offline (dine-in) menu prices, and the prices on the Platform. The Restaurant Partner agrees that this amount will be deducted from its ongoing payouts." And it explained "Price Disparity": "The Restaurant Partner will at all times maintain equal or lower prices for all its products offered to the Customers via the Platform as against the prices through its direct channels"

READ. https://www.reuters.com/world/india/indian-food-delivery-giant-zomato-drops-pricing-clause-after-pushback-source-2026-04-23/

items.description_html:
RT Govindraj Ethiraj
Fuel prices not raised for 4 years.
But not raising prices now with crude > $90-100 for weeks is sending wrong signals encouraging consumption when curtailing is need of hour.
Most analysts are saying petrol is underpriced by Rs 18-20 and diesel by Rs 30-35.
This is the math.
Whether Govt raises prices or not is a policy call.
But not moderating consumption during an energy shock will create more challenges later.

items.description_html:
RT Ira Dugal
While the world’s attention has moved to stablecoins, India is quietly chipping away at its CBDC.

At least 10 pilot programs are underway to route welfare payments - climate aid to food subsidies - via CBDC.

At the location of one such pilot, farmers said it helped them avoid upfront payments, merchants said it has improved demand, and officials said it helped in better targeting.

A super story by @kalrajs23 and @AshwinM_

items.description_html:
Choice International: Guidance - Concall Update

Management targets a medium-term annual growth rate of around 30% for both revenue and profitability.

For the AMC business, the AUM target for the close of FY '27 is INR1,000 crore.

The insurance distribution segment aims for a 50/50 mix between corporate and retail in the future. -

For the India Post Payments Bank partnership, the expected average SIP ticket size is INR2,000 to INR2,500 per customer per month.

The advisory segment's current order book is INR698 crore, with bids submitted for over INR400 crore in the pipeline. - Concall Update

Management expects the revenue mix over the next three years to be approximately 50% for Broking & Distribution, 40% for Advisory, and 10% for NBFC.

Management expects momentum to continue in FY '27, with no major impact from market volatility unless significant geopolitical issues arise.

items.title: Pre-Market Update!! China expansions, billion-dollar bids, mega mergers, fresh orders & global subsidiaries - Indian markets are firing on all cylinde...
items.description_html:
Pre-Market Update!!

China expansions, billion-dollar bids, mega mergers, fresh orders & global subsidiaries - Indian markets are firing on all cylinders

• Himadri Speciality Chemical has received board approval to establish a wholly owned step-down subsidiary in Guangzhou, China - under existing WOS AAT Global Ltd - to trade chemicals, machinery, and equipment, bolstering its global footprint.
#HSCL

• Godavari Power (GPIL)'s arm Godawari New Energy Pvt. Ltd. has inked a supply pact with Shanghai Shenyi Roche Energy Technology for Balance of System components for a 5 MWh DC Block, supporting its ambitious 20 GWh BESS facility in Sambhajinagar, Maharashtra.
#GPIL

• Bharti Airtel is reportedly in advanced talks to divest an 85% stake in its insurance vertical to Prudential. The insurance business is anticipated to be valued between ₹70 billion and ₹80 billion, signalling a major strategic divestiture.
#BhartiAirtel

• Sun Pharma is preparing to submit a binding acquisition offer worth approximately USD 13 billion for Organon, potentially marking one of the largest overseas takeover bids ever pursued by an Indian pharmaceutical enterprise.
#SunPharma

• Gujarat Themis Biosyn has announced the acquisition of Sanofi's anti-tuberculosis portfolio for €158 million, a landmark deal enabling forward integration and significantly strengthening the company's presence across global pharmaceutical markets.
#GujaratThemis

• Balrampur Chini Mills has revised the capital expenditure for its 80,000 TPA Poly Lactic Acid project upward to ₹3,080 crore from the earlier estimate of ₹2,850 crore, reflecting evolving project scope and cost dynamics.
#BalrampurChini

• Swan Defence & Triumph Offshore (TOPL) are set to merge via an NCLT-sanctioned scheme, with TOPL dissolving post-amalgamation. Shareholders will receive 1,325 SDHIL Redeemable Preference Shares per 1,000 TOPL equity shares held.
#SwanDefence

• Eveready Industries has inaugurated India's first indigenous alkaline battery manufacturing facility, aiming to scale its domestic market share from 16% to 25%. The move underscores the company's commitment to import substitution and strengthening local production capabilities.
#EvereadyIndustries

• L&T (Buildings & Factories) has secured significant orders worth ₹1,000–2,500 crore for its Buildings & Factories division, encompassing seven high-rise residential towers in Gurugram for Oberoi Realty and an advanced vehicle testing proving ground in Haryana.

• Patel Engineering has been declared the lowest bidder (L1) by SJVN Lower Arun Power Development Company for Package 2 of the 669 MW Lower Arun Hydropower Project in Nepal, with the contract valued at ₹1,593.08 crore.
#PatelEngineering

• Cupid Ltd (Karex Impact) Malaysia's Karex is signalling 30%-plus price hikes amid global supply disruptions in the health products segment. This presents a compelling margin-expansion opportunity for efficient Indian exporters, with Cupid Ltd emerging as a key potential beneficiary.
#Cupid

• J. Kumar Infraprojects has secured two new infrastructure orders in Mumbai cumulatively worth ₹2,487.65 crore, further consolidating its stronghold in the metropolitan construction and urban infrastructure development space.

• NHC Foods Limited has incorporated NHC International UK Limited as a wholly owned subsidiary in the United Kingdom, strategically positioning itself to deepen business operations across European markets and accelerate its global expansion trajectory.
#NHCFoods

items.title: Tata Capital delivered a mixed Q4 FY26 with strong top-line loan growth of 20.8% and margin expansion beating cost-to-income guidance, but missed ROA/...
items.description_html:
Tata Capital delivered a mixed Q4 FY26 with strong top-line loan growth of 20.8% and margin expansion beating cost-to-income guidance, but missed ROA/ROE/PAT targets.

The financing segment margin inflections to 25.1% and improved capital adequacy to 18.96% are positives, while asset quality deterioration (GNPA 2.61%, NNPA 1.15%) remains a concern. Execution is good with operating leverage visible, but profitability guidance misses temper enthusiasm.

Positive Factors

Loan book grew 20.8% YoY to ₹2,68,203 Cr, meeting upper end of 18-20% guidance

NII margin expanded to 46.4% from 43.0% YoY, indicating margin expansion

Cost-to-income ratio improved to 34.8% from 39.4% YoY, beating 38-39% guidance by 320-420 bps

Financing segment margin expanded to 25.1% from 21.4% YoY, a 370 bps improvement

PPOP grew 21.0% YoY to ₹9,585 Cr, demonstrating strong operating leverage

CAR improved to 18.96% from 16.80% YoY, equity grew 37.6% to ₹47,107 Cr

PAT grew 32.2% YoY to ₹4,846 Cr with normalized growth of 33.1%

Risk Factors

ROA of 1.80% missed guidance of 2.0-2.1% by 20-30 bps

ROE of 12.28% missed guidance of 13-14% by 72-172 bps

PAT growth of 32.2% missed ~35% guidance target

GNPA deteriorated to 2.61% from 2.33% YoY, up 28 bps

NNPA at 1.15% missed <1.0% guidance, up 17 bps YoY

Investment Activity segment swung to loss of ₹95.88
Cr in Q4 from profit in Q3

items.title: Adani Energy Solutions reported mixed Q4 FY26 results with strong 16.8% revenue growth driven by Smart Meter segment (582% YoY), but profitability dec...
items.description_html:
Adani Energy Solutions reported mixed Q4 FY26 results with strong 16.8% revenue growth driven by Smart Meter segment (582% YoY), but profitability declined as expenses grew faster (24.9% YoY). The Smart Meter business is emerging as a new growth engine with 18x EBIT growth, achieving the 1 crore meter target ahead of schedule.

However, margin compression of 240 bps and missed capex guidance (₹14,482 Cr vs ₹16,000-18,000 Cr guided) indicate execution challenges. The company maintains strong cash conversion (4.6x PAT) but is in a heavy capex cycle with negative free cash flow.

Positive Factors

Smart Meter segment revenue grew 582% YoY and EBIT grew 1457% YoY, becoming new growth driver

Revenue grew 16.8% YoY to ₹7,443 Cr with all business segments contributing

Achieved 1 crore cumulative smart meters target ahead of schedule

Transmission segment EBIT grew 9.4% YoY with robust 35.5% margin

Operating cash flow at 4.6x PAT indicates high-quality earnings

System availability maintained at 99.77% in Q4 FY26

Distribution losses improved to 4.20% from 4.31% YoY

Risk Factors

Total expenses grew 24.9% YoY outpacing revenue growth of 16.8%

EBITDA margin compressed 240 bps YoY to 29.9%

PBT declined 6.6% YoY to ₹910 Cr due to cost pressure

FY26 capex of ₹14,482 Cr missed guidance of ₹16,000-18,000 Cr by 9%

Other expenses increased sharply by 81.9% YoY

Debt-equity ratio worsened to 1.84x from 1.73x

Current ratio deteriorated to 1.15x from 1.46x

items.title: HSCL delivered strong FY26 with PAT growth of 35.2% and margin expansion of 292 bps, meeting its margin sustainability target. The new capacity (70,00...
items.description_html:
HSCL delivered strong FY26 with PAT growth of 35.2% and margin expansion of 292 bps, meeting its margin sustainability target.

The new capacity (70,000 MTPA speciality carbon black line) and acquisitions (Birla Tyres) provide near-term growth catalysts, though volume impact was not visible in Q4. Cash flow deterioration due to working capital and capex is a near-term concern but aligns with the planned investment cycle for future growth.

Positive Factors

PAT grew 35.2% YoY to ₹751.34 Cr in FY26, on track for FY28 doubling goal

EBITDA margin expanded 292 bps YoY to 20.4% driven by operational efficiency and high value-added products

FY26 capex of ₹636.64 Cr executed within guided ₹600-700 Cr range

New Others segment (acquisitions) scaled rapidly to ₹157.92 Cr revenue in Q4 with 16.1% EBIT margin

Core Carbon materials EBIT margin expanded 374 bps to 19.0% despite flat revenue

Company migrated to lower tax regime (Sec 115BAA) reducing ETR from 31.1% to 24.6%

Risk Factors

Operating cash flow declined 14.5% YoY to ₹382 Cr due to ₹370 Cr working capital outflow

Free cash flow negative at ₹254.64 Cr as company is in heavy investment cycle

Net cash position reduced from ₹367.28 Cr to ₹93.68 Cr with borrowings doubling to ₹766.56 Cr

Core Carbon materials segment revenue down 0.4% YoY in Q4, volume growth not yet visible

Forex loss of ₹38.13 Cr in Q4 FY26 vs gain in prior year

items.title: Khaitan Chemicals reported strong FY26 full-year performance with 39.1% revenue growth and PBT turning positive from losses. However, Q4 showed a shar...
items.description_html:
Khaitan Chemicals reported strong FY26 full-year performance with 39.1% revenue growth and PBT turning positive from losses. However, Q4 showed a sharp sequential decline with 27.4% QoQ revenue drop and 37.1% YoY PAT decline.

The Fertilizers segment turnaround is confirmed with 591 bps margin expansion, but the Chemicals segment faces margin pressure. Balance sheet strengthened with Debt/Equity improving to 1.04x, though cash conversion remains weak with CFO/PAT at 0.64x due to working capital blockage. No management guidance was provided for comparison.

Positive Factors

FY26 revenue grew 39.1% YoY to ₹1,00,162.94 Lakhs

Fertilizers segment EBIT grew 406.2% YoY with margin expansion of 591 bps

Debt reduced by 3.7% YoY, Debt/Equity improved from 1.40x to 1.04x

Equity base expanded 30.5% YoY to ₹29,064.88 Lakhs

Free cash flow positive at ₹3,531.28 Lakhs for FY26

PBT swung to profit of ₹7,172.75 Lakhs in FY26 from loss of ₹1,649.98 Lakhs in FY25

Risk Factors

Q4 PAT from continuing operations declined 37.1% YoY to ₹542.39 Lakhs

Revenue declined 27.4% QoQ in Q4 showing sharp momentum loss

EBITDA margin contracted 300 bps QoQ from 12.2% to 9.2%

Working capital blockage: Other current assets increased by ₹6,167.86 Lakhs

CFO to PAT ratio weak at 0.64x indicating poor cash conversion

Chemicals segment EBIT margin compressed 446 bps YoY to 9.3%

items.title: ABSLAMC reported mixed Q4 results with 6.85% YoY revenue growth but 17.96% PAT decline due to MTM losses in Other Income. While EBITDA margin expanded...
items.description_html:
ABSLAMC reported mixed Q4 results with 6.85% YoY revenue growth but 17.96% PAT decline due to MTM losses in Other Income. While EBITDA margin expanded 126 bps to 58.14% demonstrating operating leverage, the full-year revenue missed guidance (9.5% vs 10-12% target).

The company maintains strong cash generation (0.83x CFO/PAT) and recommended healthy dividends, but faces headwinds from market share decline in SIP flows and recent talent departures in key segments.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 6.85% YoY to ₹458.23 crore in Q4

EBITDA grew 9.22% YoY to ₹266.42 crore with 126 bps margin expansion

Operating PBT (normalized) grew 8.2% YoY excluding MTM losses

CFO to PAT conversion strong at 0.83x with FCF of ₹764.44 crore

Net worth grew 8.4% YoY to ₹4,041.66 crore

Recommended dividend of ₹25.50 per share

Risk Factors

Revenue missed FY26 guidance of 10-12%, delivering 9.5% growth

PAT declined 17.96% YoY due to ₹105 crore negative swing in Other Income

Other Income swung to loss of ₹32.86 crore from gain of ₹71.96 crore

PAT growth of 4.8% lagged revenue growth of 9.5% for FY26

Subsidiary operations reported net loss of ₹10.62 crore for FY26

Departure of Heads of Passives and Alternate Investments in Q4

items.title: Union Bank delivered a solid Q4 with 6.6% YoY PAT growth driven by 74.7% YoY collapse in NPA provisions and 6.9% reduction in operating expenses. Asse...
items.description_html:
Union Bank delivered a solid Q4 with 6.6% YoY PAT growth driven by 74.7% YoY collapse in NPA provisions and 6.9% reduction in operating expenses. Asset quality reached multi-year lows (GNPA 2.82%, NNPA 0.48%) with strong PCR of 95.03%. Corporate Banking segment was a standout with 43.8% PBT growth and 600bps margin expansion, successfully managing the ₹30,000 crore book churn.

While NII declined 1.1% YoY (margin pressure materialized as guided) and Treasury operations weak, ROA target of >1% was met and credit costs stayed within 20-40bps guidance. The proactive ₹700 crore provision for new ECL norms and strong capital position (CRAR 18.10%) provide balance sheet resilience.

Positive Factors

PPOP grew 3.3% YoY to ₹7,955 crore driven by 6.9% reduction in Operating Expenses

Net profit grew 6.6% YoY to ₹5,316 crore with NPA provisions down 74.7% YoY

Corporate Banking PBT surged 43.8% YoY with margins expanding from 13.7% to 19.6%

Retail Banking PBT grew 20.0% YoY with margins improving from 19.5% to 22.3%

GNPA improved to 2.82% and NNPA to 0.48%, both at multi-year lows

CRAR at 18.10% and CET1 at 15.69% provide strong capital position

One-time proactive provision of ₹700 crore

strengthens balance sheet for new ECL norms

Risk Factors

NII declined 1.1% YoY indicating ongoing margin pressure from rate environment

Treasury Operations PBT fell 34.1% YoY with revenue down 20.0% YoY

Deposit growth of 2.7% YoY lagged behind advances growth of 10.5% YoY

Full year PPOP declined 8.0% YoY impacted by NII pressure and higher opex earlier in the year

NIM target of 2.76% was not met - margin compression materialized as guided

items.title: RAJESH delivered strong FY26 results beating its own 40% CAGR guidance on revenue (51.9% YoY), EBITDA (59.7% YoY), and PAT (48.2% YoY). Margin expande...
items.description_html:
RAJESH delivered strong FY26 results beating its own 40% CAGR guidance on revenue (51.9% YoY), EBITDA (59.7% YoY), and PAT (48.2% YoY). Margin expanded 59 bps to 12.15% demonstrating operating leverage.

However, severe cash conversion issues emerged with negative CFO of ₹(4,142) Lakhs driven by working capital stress (receivables +92% YoY, inventories +99% YoY). New BESS business entry provides a future growth catalyst. The beat on guidance is confirmed but execution quality is tempered by cash flow concerns.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 51.9% YoY to ₹1,627.94 Cr, exceeding 40% CAGR guidance

PAT grew 48.2% YoY to ₹143.20 Cr, exceeding 40% CAGR guidance

EBITDA grew 59.7% YoY to ₹197.80 Cr, exceeding 40% CAGR guidance

EBITDA margin expanded 59 bps YoY to 12.15%

Finance costs declined 27.4% YoY to ₹10.90 Cr, improving profitability

H2 execution confirmed at 60.8% of annual revenue vs guided 55-60%

New BESS business driver with 65MW/130MWh project and subsidiary formed

Risk Factors

CFO deeply negative at ₹(4,142) Lakhs vs PAT of ₹14,320 Lakhs - severe cash conversion issue

Trade receivables grew 91.9% YoY vs revenue growth of 51.9%

Inventories grew 98.8% YoY, indicating working capital stress

Cash and cash equivalents fell sharply from ₹2,218 Lakhs to ₹135 Lakhs

Working capital blockage of over ₹24,400 Lakhs from receivables and inventory

Dependence on government contracts remains high with no diversification visible

items.title: Strait of Hormuz is open during ceasefire, Iran says. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said the Strait of Hormuz was open following a ceasefire...
items.description_html:
Strait of Hormuz is open during ceasefire, Iran says.
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said the Strait of Hormuz was open following a ceasefire accord agreed in Lebanon, while US President Donald Trump said he believed a deal to end the Iran war would come "soon," although the timing remains unclear.
Araghchi said in a post on X the Strait was open for all commercial vessels for the remainder of the U.S.-brokered 10-day truce between Israeli forces and Iran-backed Hezbollah agreed between Israel and Lebanon.
He said the passage of ships would need to be along the route that Iran's Ports and Maritime Organisation had announced.
@Reuters

items.title: RT Aaron Levie: If you read this and don’t understand why it’s happening it’s an opportunity to reset your understanding of how the real world work...
items.description_html:
RT Aaron Levie
If you read this and don’t understand why it’s happening it’s an opportunity to reset your understanding of how the real world works.

The real world will need a ton of help actually getting agents going in the enterprise. Companies have legacy tech stacks they need to modernize, data in tons of fragmented tools, knowledge that isn’t captured or digitized, and change management needed to actually utilize agents effectively. And they have to do all this while still running their business day-to-day, unlike startups.

This is why there is so much opportunity for companies (software or services) to actually deploy agents in specific domains and workflows. This remains a big opportunity for both existing services providers but also tons of new startups as well. Every new technology wave produces a new era of consulting firms that can deliver on that technology.

It’s also why the FDE model is going to be alive and well for a long time because companies will want to have their vendor actually help drive the change management and implementation for their new workflows.

The people aren’t going away. Far from it.


First Squawk: OPENAI WORKING WITH CONSULTING FIRMS, INCLUDING ACCENTURE, CAPGEMINI AND PWC, TO HELP SELL CODEX TO BUSINESSES- WSJ

Win win

items.title: RT Sridhar Vembu: I am happy to see the YCombinator wave arriving in India. Our co-founder Tony and I were in silicon valley during the original YC wa...
items.description_html:
RT Sridhar Vembu
I am happy to see the YCombinator wave arriving in India. Our co-founder Tony and I were in silicon valley during the original YC wave of companies. We learned a lot from the YC companies. We also made the conscious choice to pursue a different course.

In the ancient Bharatiya tradition of philosophical debate, I am going to offer this.

I will start with things I agree with YC on.

1. The biggest lesson anyone can learn from YC: small passionate teams can do magic. This was true way before AI coding arrived and will always be true.

2. YC is absolutely right to not over-emphasize "innovation". Doing a similar product as the bigger guys, faster and cheaper, is often the best course. Google did not invent search. OpenAI did not invent the LLM. Anthropic did not invent agentic coding.

3. YC companies tend to geographically cluster, and that can lead to subtle peer pressure and group-think. So the "laggard" founders who are not growing like a weed every week start to feel left out and eventually the result is "founder depression". And YC has counselors.

By the standards of 2007 silicon valley or even 2014 silicon valley, we were thought to be losers. Just keep that in mind.

4. Many deep tech problems like building a better, cheaper MRI machine or advanced semiconductor equipment require long focus and patient execution and lots of capital. These are endurance tests, not weekly sprints.

5. YC too often optimizes companies for "exit". That philosophy was built for and requires prolonged bubbles, which American policy has delivered, at the price of nearly wrecking the country (note the extreme inequality and political division). If you love India, you should not wish for similar bubbles.

6. YC model worked in silicon valley. One of the reasons it worked was that silicon valley could get any talent from anywhere in the world, notably from India, easily. That era may have ended or at least on pause right now.

Bengaluru has tried the same thing but with "any talent from anywhere in India" and we have not yet created huge companies. India needs its Huawei and Xiaomi and BYD and these companies are Chinese to the core, built by patriotic Chinese. Indian talent, staying in India, rooted in India, is going to have to build companies like them. Enough said.


Jared Friedman: What I told 2,000 future founders in Bengaluru today:

1/ We believe we are at the start of a second wave of Indian companies that will build world-class AI native products for the global market. Emergent and Giga are the model of the future.

2/ Just because a space seems

items.title: RT Techmeme: Sources: Google is negotiating a deal with the US DOD that would let the Pentagon deploy Gemini AI models in classified settings, reversi...

items.title: EMDE policy responses to the Fed & dollar have served them well in past crises. Many EMDE central banks will retain this approach, prioritizing contai...
items.description_html:
EMDE policy responses to the Fed & dollar have served them well in past crises. Many EMDE central banks will retain this approach, prioritizing containing inflation over maintaining short-term growth by tracking US monetary policy & exchange rates.

items.title: Reviving a modern helium reserve would not eliminate risk. But it would ensure that when the next disruption comes, the world is not left scrambling f...
items.description_html: Reviving a modern helium reserve would not eliminate risk. But it would ensure that when the next disruption comes, the world is not left scrambling for a gas that, while invisible, underpins the technologies we depend on every day. https://www.piie.com/blogs/realtime-economics/2026/bring-back-helium-reserve-next-shock-hits

items.title: It has been a common belief that somehow "offshore" dollars, including the dollar reserves held by non-US authorities, have to "leave" the US through ...
items.description_html:
It has been a common belief that somehow "offshore" dollars, including the dollar reserves held by non-US authorities, have to "leave" the US through US current account deficits. This belief is dead wrong.
Maurice Obstfeld explains: https://www.piie.com/blogs/realtime-economics/2026/dont-blame-americas-current-account-deficit-dollar

items.title: Brazil's October election is a contest over whether Brazil controls terms of the world accessing its resources or if terms are set by others, & will s...
items.description_html: Brazil's October election is a contest over whether Brazil controls terms of the world accessing its resources or if terms are set by others, & will shape critical mineral supply chains, climate governance, & the Americas' balance of power for a generation.https://www.piie.com/blogs/realtime-economics/2026/why-foreign-policy-central-question-brazils-next-election

items.title: The main drivers of the US large & persistent current account deficits are the unsustainable US federal budget outlook & China's failure to shift its ...

items.title: The US used to have a Federal Helium Reserve but sold it & the infrastructure in 2024. The government should complement its Project Vault program with...
items.description_html:
The US used to have a Federal Helium Reserve but sold it & the infrastructure in 2024.
The government should complement its Project Vault program with a special helium reserve tailored to its unique storage, rotation, & allocation requirements.
https://www.piie.com/blogs/realtime-economics/2026/bring-back-helium-reserve-next-shock-hits

items.title: RT The Atlantic: The effects of the Iran war may compel some countries to stockpile huge amounts of energy, and cause some developing countries to dis...
items.description_html:
RT The Atlantic
The effects of the Iran war may compel some countries to stockpile huge amounts of energy, and cause some developing countries to distrust the U.S. more broadly, Adam Posen tells @davidfrum: “It's not a good legacy, even before you get to the foreign policy.”

Watch "The David Frum Show": https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BH0H4dgmmq0
